model_narrative: We plan production across several plants that can each run a number of processes turning raw materials into intermediate goods and ultimately into final products. For every process at every plant we decide the level to run it at, and we decide how much of each raw material to purchase from outside. Running processes draws on shared equipment with limited capacity, and the activity at each plant exposes the operation to various labor unions. We also decide which unions end up involved. The objective is to minimize the total number of unions involved.

sets.name: commodity
sets.doc: the commodities flowing through the system, both intermediate goods and final products

sets.name: process
sets.doc: the production processes that can be run at a plant

sets.name: unit
sets.doc: the physical capacity units, the equipment groups whose throughput is limited

sets.name: union
sets.doc: the labor unions whose involvement is being decided

sets.name: rawmat
sets.doc: the subset of commodities that are raw materials and can therefore be purchased from outside the system

params.name: demand
params.doc: the required net amount of each commodity, in millions of units; commodities with no external requirement have demand zero

params.name: a
params.doc: the input-output matrix; the net amount of a commodity produced per unit of a process when positive, or consumed per unit when negative, in millions of units

params.name: b
params.doc: the capacity utilization matrix; how much of a capacity unit is used per unit of a process run

params.name: k80
params.doc: the available capacity of each unit at each plant as of 1980, in millions of units; a plant that does not have a given unit has capacity zero there

params.name: emp
params.doc: the employment tied to each plant for each union, in thousands

params.name: ur
params.doc: the union exposure coefficient linking a process at a plant to a union, derived from how much that union is employed where the process can run

params.name: mu
params.doc: the maximum possible union exposure for each union, the largest total exposure that union could ever reach across all processes and plants; used as the scaling ceiling when the union is involved

vars.name: nunion
vars.doc: the total count of unions that end up involved

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the level at which each process is run at each plant, in millions of units

vars.name: up
vars.doc: one if the union is involved, zero otherwise

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the amount of each commodity purchased from outside as raw material, in millions of units; only meaningful for commodities that are raw materials

constraints.name: a[commodity,
constraints.intent: For each commodity, the total amount produced across all processes and all plants, with consumption counted against production, together with any of that commodity bought in from outside when it is a raw material, must come out equal to the required net amount of that commodity. Commodities that are not raw materials cannot be bought in and so rely entirely on what the processes produce.

constraints.name: b[unit,
constraints.intent: For each piece of equipment at each plant, the total throughput it has to support from all the processes running at that plant must stay within the capacity that equipment has available at that plant.

constraints.name: mu[union])
constraints.intent: For each union, the total activity that exposes the operation to that union, accumulated over every process at every plant, is only allowed up to that union's maximum possible exposure, and only when the union is marked as involved. If the union is not involved, none of that activity is permitted. A union that has no possible exposure at all places no restriction.

constraints.name: nunion
constraints.intent: The total count of unions involved must equal the number of unions that are marked as involved.

query: Suppose both the Machinists and the Electrical Workers end up signed on together. With IAM and IBEW both in, where does the union headcount land?
